And how could He be described, when He said in His Book: "They have not appraised Allah with the truth of His appraisal" (6:91)? He cannot be described by any measure, for He is greater than that. And the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) cannot be described. And how could a servant be described whom Allah the Almighty and Majestic has veiled with seven [heavens], and whose obedience on earth He has made like His obedience in the heavens? He said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from" (59:7). And [He said]: "Whoever obeys him has obeyed Me, and whoever disobeys him has disobeyed Me," and He has entrusted the matter to him.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 102

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 30; Nur al-Thaqalayn; al-Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 182; al-Mu'min, p. 30

entries.arabic: لَا تَقُولُوا لِکُلِّ آیَهًٍْ هَذِهِ رَجُلٌ وَ هَذِهِ رَجُلٌ إِنَّ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ حَلَالًا وَ مِنْهُ حَرَاماً وَ فِیهِ نَبَأُ مَنْ قَبْلَکُمْ وَ خَبَرُ مَنْ بَعْدَکُمْ وَ حُکْمُ مَا بَیْنَکُمْ فَهَکَذَا هُوَ کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مُفَوَّضٌ فِیهِ إِنْ شَاءَ فَعَلَ الشَّیْءَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ تَذَکَّرَ حَتَّی إِذَا فُرِضَتْ فَرَائِضُهُ وَ خُمِّسَتْ أَخْمَاسُهُ حَقٌّ عَلَی النَّاسِ أَنْ یَأْخُذُوا بِهِ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: به هر آیه‌ای نگویید این مردی است و این مردی است [و از پیش خود هر آیه‌ای را به کسی نسبت ندهید]. بخشی از قرآن [مربوط به] حلال و بخشی [مربوط به] حرام است و در آن خبر کسانی که قبل از شما بودند است، و خبر کسانی که بعد از شما هستند و حکم آنچه در میان شماست و آن همین‌طور است. [قبل از نزول قرآن] در مورد آن (حلال و حرام) به رسول خدا (صلی الله علیه و آله) اختیار داده شده بود؛ اگر می‌خواست آن چیز را انجام می‌داد و اگر می‌خواست متذکّر می‌شد تا اینکه واجباتش واجب شد و خمس‌هایش گرفته شد. بر مردم شایسته است که آن را بگیرند [و بر آن عمل کنند]؛ چون خداوند فرمود: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۲
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۸۹، ص۱۱۱/ العیاشی، ج۱، ص۱۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Do not say of every verse, "This refers to such-and-such a man, and this refers to such-and-such a man." Indeed, the Quran contains what is lawful and what is unlawful, and it contains the news of those who came before you, the report of those who will come after you, and the judgment for what is between you; it is exactly as such. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) was granted authority over it; if he wished, he would perform a thing, and if he wished, he would remind [the people], until its obligatory duties were made mandatory and its fifths were established. It is incumbent upon the people to accept it [and act upon it], because Allah said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you, take it; and whatever he has forbidden you, refrain from it."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 102

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 89, p. 111 / al-Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 18

entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِیُّ (صلی الله علیه و آله) حَاکِیاً عَنْ رَبِّهِ یَأْمُرُهُ بِهَذِهِ الْخِصَالِ قَالَ صِلْ مَنْ قَطَعَکَ وَ اعْفُ عَمَّنْ ظَلَمَکَ وَ أَعْطِ مَنْ حَرَمَکَ وَ أَحْسِنْ إِلَی مَنْ أَسَاءَ إِلَیْکَ وَ قَدْ أُمِرْنَا بِمُتَابَعَتِهِ یَقُولُ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: رسول اکرم (صلی الله علیه و آله) فرمود: «خداوند متعال او را امر کرده است تا با کسانی که با او قطع رابطه کرده‌اند ارتباط برقرار کند، و یا از کسانی که به او ظلم کرده‌اند درگذرد، و یا آن‌هایی که او را محروم کرده‌اند مورد لطف قرار دهد، و یا به کسانی که به او بد کرده‌اند نیکی نماید، و خدا به ما امر کرده که از رسولش متابعت کنیم: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۶۸، ص۴۲۳/ مصباح الشریعهًْ، ص۱۵۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said, narrating from his Lord, who commanded him with these qualities: "Connect with those who cut you off, forgive those who wronged you, give to those who deprived you, and do good to those who did evil to you." And we have been commanded to follow him, for Allah (mighty and majestic is He) says: "And whatever the Messenger has given you, take it; and whatever he has forbidden you, refrain from it."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 68, p. 423 / Misbah al-Shari'ah, p. 158

entries.arabic: إِنَّهُ مَنْ عَرَفَ دِینَهُ مِنْ کِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّوَجَلَّ زَالَتِ الْجِبَالُ قَبْلَ أَنْ یَزُولَ وَ مَنْ دَخَلَ فِی أَمْرٍ بِجَهْلٍ خَرَجَ مِنْهُ بِجَهْلٍ قُلْتُ وَ مَا هُوَ فِی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّوَجَلَّ قَالَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: محمدحلبی گوید: امام صادق (علیه السلام) به من فرمود: «هرکس دین خود را از کتاب خداوند بگیرد از کوه‌های استوار پابرجاتر است و هرکه با نادانی در کاری وارد شود به نادانی خارج می‌شود». گفتم: «این مطلب در کدام آیه قرآن است». فرمود: «این آیه: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا است».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: بشارهًْ المصطفی، ص۱۲۹/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۳، ص۱۰۲
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Whoever learns his religion from the Book of Allah, the Almighty and Majestic, will remain steadfast even if the mountains were to be moved. And whoever enters into a matter out of ignorance will depart from it in ignorance. I asked, "Where is this in the Book of Allah, the Almighty and Majestic?" He replied, "It is the saying of Allah, the Almighty and Majestic: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from.'"

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Bisharat al-Mustafa, p. 129 / Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 23, p. 102

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً (صلی الله علیه و آله) عَبْداً فَأَدَّبَهُ حَتَّی إِذَا بَلَغَ أَرْبَعِینَ سَنَهًْ أَوْحَی إِلَیْهِ وَ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ الْأَشْیَاءَ فَقَال ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: خداوند تبارک‌وتعالی در امر خلقش، اختیار را به پیامبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) عطا کرد تا ببیند خلقش چگونه او را اطاعت می‌کنند. سپس این آیه را تلاوت کرد: و مَا آتَاکُمُ الرَّسُول فَخُذُوهُ ومَا نَهَاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۷۸/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۵، ص۳۳۱
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah created Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) as a servant, and He disciplined him until, when he reached forty years of age, He revealed to him and delegated the affairs to him. He then said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 378; Nur al-Thaqalayn; al-Burhan; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 25, p. 331

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ أَدَّبَ رَسُولَهُ حَتَّی قَوَّمَهُ عَلَی مَا أَرَادَ ثُمَّ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ فَقَالَ عَزَّ ذِکْرُهُ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَمَا فَوَّضَ اللَّهُ إِلَی رَسُولِهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَقَدْ فَوَّضَهُ إِلَیْنَا.
entries.persian: خداوند پیامبرش را تربیت نمود به‌حدّی که می‌خواست برسد؛ سپس به او واگذار نمود و فرمود: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا هرچه پیامبر برای شما آورد اطاعت کنید و از هرچه بازداشت خودداری کنید هرچه خداوند به پیامبرش واگذار کرده آن جناب در اختیار ما گذاشته است.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: الکافی، ج۱، ص۲۶۸/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۶/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۵، ص۳۳۲/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۸۳/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) disciplined His Messenger until He perfected him according to what He willed. Then, He delegated [authority] to him and said (Mighty is His remembrance): "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from." Whatever Allah has delegated to His Messenger (peace be upon him and his family), he has indeed delegated to us.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 268; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 6; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 25, p. 332; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 383; Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Burhan

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَلَمَّا انْتَهَی بِهِ إِلَی مَا أَرَادَ قَالَ لَهُ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ فَفَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ دِینَهُ فَقَالَ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ فَرَضَ الْفَرَائِضَ وَ لَمْ یَقْسِمْ لِلْجَدِّ شَیْئاً وَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَطْعَمَهُ السُّدُسَ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ جَلَّ ذِکْرُهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ وَ ذَلِکَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عزّوجلّ هذا عَطاؤُنا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِکْ بِغَیْرِ حِسابٍ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: الکافی، ج۱، ص۲۶۷/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۰۱، ص۳۴۲/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۷۹/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان؛ «فوض» بدل «ففوض»/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۵
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, disciplined His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family), and when he reached the level that Allah desired for him, He said to him: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character." (68:4). Then He entrusted His religion to him and said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from." (59:7). And verily, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) ordained the obligatory shares of inheritance and did not allot anything for the grandfather. But the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) granted him a sixth, and Allah (Glorious is His mention) permitted that for him. And that is the saying of Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He): "This is Our gift, so give or withhold, without account." (38:39).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 267; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 101, p. 342; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 379; Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Burhan; "fawwada" instead of "fa-fawwada"; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 342.

entries.arabic: أَحْمَدُ‌بْنُ‌الْحَسَنِ الْمِیثَمِیُ أَنَّه‌سَأَلَ الرِّضَا (علیه السلام) یَوْماً وَ قَدِ اجْتَمَعَ عِنْدَهُ قَوْمٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ وَ قَدْ کَانُوا یَتَنَازَعُونَ فِی الْحَدِیثَیْنِ الْمُخْتَلِفَیْنِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِی الشَّیْءِ الْوَاحِدِ فَقَالَ (علیه السلام) إِنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَ حَرَاماً وَ أَحَلَّ حَلَالًا وَ فَرَضَ فَرَائِضَ فَمَا جَاءَ فِی تَحْلِیلِ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ أَوْ فِی تَحْرِیمِ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ أَوْ دَفْعِ فَرِیضَهًٍْ فِی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ رَسَمَهَا بَیْنَ قَائِمٍ بِلَا نَاسِخٍ نَسَخَ ذَلِکَ فَذَلِکَ مَا لَا یَسَعُ الْأَخْذُ بِهِ لِأَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) لَمْ یَکُنْ لِیُحَرِّمَ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ وَ لَا لِیُحَلِّلَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَ لَا لِیُغَیِّرَ فَرَائِضَ اللَّهِ وَ أَحْکَامَهُ کَانَ فِی ذَلِکَ کُلِّهِ مُتَّبِعاً مُسَلِّماً مُؤَدِّیاً عَنِ اللَّهِ وَ ذَلِکَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا ما یُوحی إِلَیَّ فَکَانَ (علیه السلام) مُتَّبِعاً لِلَّهِ مُؤَدِّیاً عَنِ اللَّهِ مَا أَمَرَهُ بِهِ مِنْ تَبْلِیغِ الرِّسَالَهًِْ قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ یَرِدُ عَنْکُمُ الْحَدِیثُ فِی الشَّیْءِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مِمَّا لَیْسَ فِی الْکِتَابِ وَ هُوَ فِی السُّنَّهًِْ ثُمَّ یَرِدُ خِلَافُهُ فَقَالَ کَذَلِکَ قَدْ نَهَی رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) عَنْ أَشْیَاءَ نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ فَوَافَقَ فِی ذَلِکَ نَهْیُهُ نَهْیَ اللَّهِ وَ أَمَرَ بِأَشْیَاءَ فَصَارَ ذَلِکَ الْأَمْرُ وَاجِباً لَازِماً کَعِدْلِ فَرَائِضِ اللَّهِ فَوَافَقَ فِی ذَلِکَ أَمْرُهُ أَمْرَ اللَّهِ فَمَا جَاءَ فِی النَّهْیِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ ثُمَّ جَاءَ خِلَافُهُ لَمْ یَسُغِ اسْتِعْمَالُ ذَلِکَ وَ کَذَلِکَ فِیمَا أَمَرَ بِهِ لِأَنَّا لَا نُرَخِّصُ فِیمَا لَمْ یُرَخِّصْ فِیهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ لَا نَأْمُرُ بِخِلَافِ مَا أَمَرَ بِهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) إِلَّا لِعِلَّهًِْ خَوْفِ ضَرُورَهًٍْ فَأَمَّا أَنْ نَسْتَحِلَّ مَا حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَوْ نُحَرِّمَ مَا اسْتَحَلَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَلَا یَکُونُ ذَلِکَ أَبَداً لِأَنَّا تَابِعُونَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مُسَلِّمُونَ لَهُ کَمَا کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) تَابِعاً لِأَمْرِ رَبِّهِ مُسَلِّماً لَهُ وَ قَالَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ نَهَی عَنْ أَشْیَاءَ لَیْسَ نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ بَلْ إِعَافَهًٍْ وَ کَرَاهَهًٍْ وَ أَمَرَ بِأَشْیَاءَ لَیْسَ بِأَمْرِ فَرْضٍ وَ لَا وَاجِبٍ بَلْ أَمْرِ فَضْلٍ وَ رُجْحَانٍ فِی الدِّینِ ثُمَّ رَخَّصَ فِی ذَلِکَ لِلْمَعْلُولِ وَ غَیْرِ الْمَعْلُولِ فَمَا کَانَ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) نَهْیَ إِعَافَهًٍْ أَوْ أَمْرَ فَضْلٍ فَذَلِکَ الَّذِی یَسَعُ اسْتِعْمَالُ الرُّخْصَهًِْ فِیهِ إِذَا وَرَدَ عَلَیْکُمْ عَنَّا الْخَبَرُ فِیهِ بِاتِّفَاقٍ یَرْوِیهِ مَنْ یَرْوِیهِ فِی النَّهْیِ وَ لَا یُنْکِرُهُ وَ کَانَ الْخَبَرَانِ صَحِیحَیْنِ مَعْرُوفَیْنِ بِاتِّفَاقِ النَّاقِلَهًِْ فِیهِمَا یَجِبُ الْأَخْذُ بِأَحَدِهِمَا أَوْ بِهِمَا جَمِیعاً أَوْ بِأَیِّهِمَا شِئْتَ وَ أَحْبَبْتَ مُوَسَّعٌ ذَلِکَ لَکَ مِنْ بَابِ التَّسْلِیمِ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ الرَّدِّ إِلَیْهِ وَ إِلَیْنَا وَ کَانَ تَارِکُ ذَلِکَ مِنْ بَابِ الْعِنَادِ وَ الْإِنْکَارِ وَ تَرْکِ التَّسْلِیمِ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مُشْرِکاً بِاللَّهِ الْعَظِیمِ فَمَا وَرَدَ عَلَیْکُمْ مِنْ خَبَرَیْنِ مُخْتَلِفَیْنِ فَاعْرِضُوهُمَا عَلَی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ فَمَا کَانَ فِی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ مَوْجُوداً حَلَالًا أَوْ حَرَاماً فَاتَّبِعُوا مَا وَافَقَ الْکِتَابَ وَ مَا لَمْ یَکُنْ فِی الْکِتَابِ فَاعْرِضُوهُ عَلَی سُنَنِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَمَا کَانَ فِی السُّنَّهًِْ مَوْجُوداً مَنْهِیّاً عَنْهُ نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ وَ مَأْمُوراً بِهِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمْرَ إِلْزَامٍ فَاتَّبِعُوا مَا وَافَقَ نَهْیَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ أَمْرَهُ وَ مَا کَانَ فِی السُّنَّهًِْ نَهْیَ إِعَافَهًٍْ أَوْ کَرَاهَهًٍْ ثُمَّ کَانَ الْخَبَرُ الْأَخِیرُ خِلَافَهُ فَذَلِکَ رُخْصَهًٌْ فِیمَا عَافَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ کَرِهَهُ وَ لَمْ یُحَرِّمْهُ فَذَلِکَ الَّذِی یَسَعُ الْأَخْذُ بِهِمَا جَمِیعاً وَ بِأَیِّهِمَا شِئْتَ وَسِعَکَ الِاخْتِیَارُ مِنْ بَابِ التَّسْلِیمِ وَ الِاتِّبَاعِ وَ الرَّدِّ إِلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ مَا لَمْ تَجِدُوهُ فِی شَیْءٍ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْوُجُوهِ فَرُدُّوا إِلَیْنَا عِلْمَهُ فَنَحْنُ أَوْلَی بِذَلِکَ وَ لَا تَقُولُوا فِیهِ بِآرَائِکُمْ وَ عَلَیْکُمْ بِالْکَفِّ وَ التَّثَبُّتِ وَ الْوُقُوفِ وَ أَنْتُمْ طَالِبُونَ بَاحِثُونَ حَتَّی یَأْتِیَکُمُ الْبَیَانُ مِنْ عِنْدِنَا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: وسایل الشیعهًْ، ج۲۷، ص۱۱۳/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲، ص۲۳۳ و عیون أخبارالرضا (ج۲، ص۲۰؛ «اذا ورد علیکم عنا الخبر ... الی آخر» محذوف/ نورالثقلین؛ «بتفاوت»
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Rida (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Ahmad ibn al-Hasan al-Maythami reported that one day, while a group of his companions were gathered around him, they were disputing two conflicting hadiths from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) regarding a single matter. He (peace be upon him) said: "Allah has forbidden what is forbidden and made lawful what is lawful, and He has ordained obligations. Therefore, whatever comes regarding the making lawful of what Allah has forbidden, or the forbidding of what Allah has made lawful, or the setting aside of an obligation that Allah has established in His Book—without a clear abrogator (nasikh) having abrogated it—then that is something which cannot be accepted. For the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) would not forbid what Allah has made lawful, nor make lawful what Allah has forbidden, nor change the obligations and decrees of Allah. In all of this, he was a follower, a submitter, and a conveyor on behalf of Allah. This is the meaning of Allah’s saying: 'I follow only what is revealed to me' (6:9). Thus, he (peace be upon him and his family) was a follower of Allah, conveying on behalf of Allah what He commanded him to convey of the Message."

I said: "But a hadith reaches us from you regarding a matter from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) that is not in the Book, but is in the Sunnah, and then its opposite reaches us."

He said: "It is the same. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) forbade certain things with a prohibition of unlawfulness (tahrim), and in that, his prohibition matched the prohibition of Allah. And he commanded certain things, and that command became obligatory and binding, like the obligations of Allah, and in that, his command matched the command of Allah. So, whatever comes from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) as a prohibition of unlawfulness, and then its opposite comes, it is not permissible to act upon it. And it is the same regarding what he commanded, for we do not permit what the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) did not permit, nor do we command the opposite of what the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) commanded, unless there is a cause of fear or necessity. As for us declaring lawful what the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) forbade, or forbidding what the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) declared lawful, that shall never happen, for we are followers of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family), submitting to him, just as the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) was a follower of the command of his Lord, submitting to Him. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you, abstain from it' (59:7).

"And Allah forbade certain things, not as a prohibition of unlawfulness, but as a matter of dislike and aversion (karahah), and He commanded certain things, not as a command of obligation or duty, but as a matter of excellence and preference in religion. Then He granted a concession (rukhsah) in that for the one who is excused and the one who is not. So, whatever comes from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) as a prohibition of aversion or a command of excellence, that is where it is permissible to utilize the concession. When a report reaches you from us regarding this, with consensus—narrated by those who narrate it regarding the prohibition, and they do not deny it—and both reports are authentic and known by the consensus of the transmitters, then it is necessary to take one of them, or both of them together, or whichever you wish and prefer. This is made broad for you as a matter of submission to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) and referring back to him and to us. But whoever abandons this out of stubbornness, denial, and failure to submit to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) is a polytheist toward Allah the Great.

"So, whatever conflicting reports reach you, present them to the Book of Allah. Whatever is found in the Book of Allah as lawful or forbidden, follow what agrees with the Book. And whatever is not in the Book, present it to the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family). Whatever is found in the Sunnah as forbidden with a prohibition of unlawfulness, or commanded by the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) as a command of obligation, follow what agrees with the prohibition and command of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family). And whatever is in the Sunnah as a prohibition of aversion or dislike, and then the later report is its opposite, that is a concession regarding what the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) disliked and found averse, but did not forbid. That is where it is permissible to take both of them, or whichever you wish; you have the choice as a matter of submission, following, and referring back to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family). And whatever you do not find in any of these ways, refer its knowledge to us; we are more entitled to that. Do not speak about it based on your own opinions, and you must refrain, verify, and pause, while you are seekers and researchers, until the clarification comes to you from us."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 104

References: Wasa'il al-Shi'ah, Vol. 27, p. 113; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 2, p. 233; 'Uyun Akhbar al-Rida (Vol. 2, p. 20); Nur al-Thaqalayn

entries.arabic: ابْنِ نُبَاتَهًَْ قَالَ سَأَلْتُ أَمِیرَالْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) عَنْ سَلْمَانَ الْفَارِسِیِّ (رحمة الله علیه) وَ قُلْتُ مَا تَقُولُ فِیهِ فَقَالَ مَا أَقُولُ فِی رَجُلٍ خُلِقَ مِنْ طینتا (طِینَتِنَا) وَ رُوحُهُ مَقْرُونَهًٌْ بِرُوحِنَا خَصَّهُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی مِنَ الْعُلُومِ بِأَوَّلِهَا وَ آخِرِهَا وَ ظَاهِرِهَا وَ بَاطِنِهَا وَ سِرِّهَا وَ عَلَانِیَتِهَا وَ لَقَدْ حَضَرْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ سَلْمَانُ (رحمة الله علیه) بَیْنَ یَدَیْهِ فَدَخَلَ أَعْرَابِیٌّ فَنَحَّاهُ عَنْ مَکَانِهِ وَ جَلَسَ فِیهِ فَغَضِبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله)حَتَّی دَرَّ الْعَرَقُ بَیْنَ عَیْنَیْهِ وَ احْمَرَّتَا عَیْنَاهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ أَ تُنَحِّی رَجُلًا یُحِبُّهُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی فِی السَّمَاءِ وَ یُحِبُّهُ رَسُولُهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِی الْأَرْضِ یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ أَ‌تُنَحِّی رَجُلًا مَا حَضَرَنِی جَبْرَئِیلُ (علیه السلام) إِلَّا أَمَرَنِی عَنْ رَبِّی عزّوجلّ أَنْ أُقْرِأَهُ السَّلَامَ یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ إِنَّ سَلْمَانَ (رحمة الله علیه) مِنِّی مَنْ جَفَاهُ فَقَدْ جَفَانِی وَ مَنْ آذَاهُ فَقَدْ آذَانِی وَ مَنْ بَاعَدَهُ فَقَدْ بَاعَدَنِی وَ مَنْ قَرَّبَهُ فَقَدْ قَرَّبَنِی یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ لَا تَغْلَطَنَّ فِی سَلْمَانَ (رحمة الله علیه) فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی قَدْ أَمَرَنِی أَنْ أُطْلِعَهُ عَلَی عِلْمِ الْمَنَایَا وَ الْبَلَایَا وَ الْأَنْسَابِ وَ فَصْلِ الْخِطَابِ قَالَ فَقَالَ الْأَعْرَابِیُّ یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مَا ظَنَنْتُ أَنْ یَبْلُغَ مِنْ فِعْلِ سَلْمَانَ (رحمة الله علیه) مَا ذَکَرْتَ أَ لَیْسَ کَانَ مَجُوسِیّاً ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَ فَقَالَ النَّبِیُّ (صلی الله علیه و آله) یَا أَعْرَابِیٌّ أُخَاطِبُکَ عَنْ رَبِّی وَ تُقَاوِلُنِی إِنَّ سَلْمَانَ (رحمة الله علیه) مَا کَانَ مَجُوسِیّاً وَ لَکِنَّهُ کَانَ مُظْهِراً لِلشِّرْکِ مُبْطِناً لِلْإِیمَانِ یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ أَ مَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ یَقُولُ فَلا وَ رَبِّکَ لا یُؤْمِنُونَ حَتَّی یُحَکِّمُوکَ فِیما شَجَرَ بَیْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ لا یَجِدُوا فِی أَنْفُسِهِمْ حَرَجاً مِمَّا قَضَیْتَ وَ یُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِیماً أَ مَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ یَقُولُ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا یَا أَعْرَابِیُّ خُذْ ما آتَیْتُکَ وَ کُنْ مِنَ الشَّاکِرِینَ وَ لَا تَجْحَدْ فَتَکُونَ مِنَ الْمُعَذَّبِینَ وَ سَلِّمْ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) قَوْلَهُ تَکُنْ مِنَ الْآمِنِینَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۰۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Ibn Nubata said: I asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) about Salman al-Farsi (may God have mercy on him) and said, "What do you say about him?" He replied, "What should I say about a man who was created from our clay (tinatina), and whose soul is linked to our soul? God, Blessed and Exalted is He, has singled him out from among the sciences with their beginning and their end, their outward and their inward, their secret and their manifest. I was in the presence of the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) while Salman (may God have mercy on him) was sitting before him. A Bedouin entered, pushed him from his place, and sat in it. The Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) became angry until sweat flowed between his eyes and his eyes turned red. Then he said, 'O Bedouin! Do you push aside a man whom God, Blessed and Exalted is He, loves in the heavens and whom His Messenger (peace be upon him and his family) loves on the earth? O Bedouin! Do you push aside a man such that Gabriel (peace be upon him) never came to me without commanding me from my Lord, the Almighty and Majestic, to convey greetings of peace to him? O Bedouin! Salman (may God have mercy on him) is of me; whoever treats him with hostility has treated me with hostility, and whoever harms him has harmed me, and whoever distances him has distanced me, and whoever draws him near has drawn me near. O Bedouin! Do not be mistaken about Salman (may God have mercy on him), for God, Blessed and Exalted is He, has commanded me to inform him of the knowledge of deaths, calamities, lineages, and the decisive judgment (fasl al-khitab).' The Bedouin said, 'O Messenger of God! I did not think that the status of Salman (may God have mercy on him) reached what you have mentioned. Was he not a Magian, and then he became a Muslim?' The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said, 'O Bedouin! I am speaking to you on behalf of my Lord, and you argue with me? Salman (may God have mercy on him) was not a Magian; rather, he was one who manifested polytheism while concealing faith. O Bedouin! Have you not heard God, the Almighty and Majestic, say: "But no, by your Lord, they will not [truly] believe until they make you, [O Muhammad], judge concerning that over which they dispute among themselves and then find within themselves no discomfort from what you have judged and submit in [full, willing] submission" (4:65)? Have you not heard God, the Almighty and Majestic, say: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from" (59:7)? O Bedouin! Take what I have given you and be among the grateful. Do not deny it, lest you be among those who are punished. Submit to the word of the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family), and you shall be among those who are secure.'"

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 108

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 346; al-Ikhtisas, p. 221

entries.arabic: فَإِنَّ أَمْرَ النَّبِیِّ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مِثْلُ الْقُرْآنِ نَاسِخٌ وَ مَنْسُوخٌ وَ خَاصٌّ وَ عَامٌّ وَ مُحْکَمٌ وَ مُتَشَابِهٌ قَدْ کَانَ یَکُونُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) الْکَلَامُ لَهُ وَجْهَانِ کَلَامٌ عَامٌّ وَ کَلَامٌ خَاصٌّ مِثْلُ الْقُرْآنِ وَ قَالَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ فِی کِتَابِهِ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَیَشْتَبِهُ عَلَی مَنْ لَمْ یَعْرِفْ وَ لَمْ یَدْرِ مَا عَنَی اللَّهُ بِهِ وَ رَسُولُهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ لَیْسَ کُلُّ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) کَانَ یَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الشَّیْءِ فَیَفْهَمُ وَ کَانَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ یَسْأَلُهُ وَ لَا یَسْتَفْهِمُهُ حَتَّی إِنْ کَانُوا لَیُحِبُّونَ أَنْ یَجِیءَ الْأَعْرَابِیُّ وَ الطَّارِئُ فَیَسْأَلَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) حَتَّی یَسْمَعُوا.
entries.persian: امر پیغمبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) هم مانند قرآن ناسخ و منسوخ [و خاص و عام] و محکم و متشابه دارد، گاهی رسول خدا (صلی الله علیه و آله) به دو طریق سخن می‌فرمود: سخنی عام و سخنی خاص مثل قرآن. و خدای عزّوجلّ در کتابش فرموده است: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا کسی که مقصود خدا و رسولش را نفهمد و درک نکند، بر او مشتبه شود، اصحاب پیغمبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) که چیزی از او می‌پرسیدند همه متوجّه نمی‌شوند بعضی از آن‌ها از پیغمبر می‌پرسیدند ولی (به علّت شرم یا احترام یا بی‌قیدی) فهم‌جویی نمی‌کردند و دوست داشتند که بیابانی و رهگذری بیاید و از پیغمبر بپرسد تا آن‌ها بشنوند.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۰
entries.references: نورالثقلین؛ «و لیس کل اصحاب ... الی آخر» محذوف/ محذوف/ الکافی، ج۱، ص۶۴/ بحارالأنوار، ج۳۶، ص۲۷۳/ الخصال، ج۱، ص۲۵۷ و تحف العقول، ص۱۹۳؛ «حتی ان کانو الیحبون ... الی آخر» محذوف/ الغیبهًْ للنعمانی، ج۸۰/ مستدرک الوسایل، ج۱۷، ص۳۴۲/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲، ص۲۲۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: The command of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) is like the Quran; it has abrogating and abrogated [verses], specific and general [rulings], and definitive and allegorical [meanings]. Sometimes the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) would speak in two ways: a general speech and a specific speech, just like the Quran. Allah, the Almighty and Majestic, said in His Book: "Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, abstain from it." It becomes ambiguous to those who do not know and do not understand what Allah and His Messenger (peace be upon him and his family) meant by it. Not all of the companions of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) would ask him about a matter and understand it; among them were those who would ask him but not seek to comprehend it. They would even wish for a Bedouin or a newcomer to come and ask the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) so that they could listen.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 110

References: Nur al-Thaqalayn; "wa laysa kull ashab... ila al-akhir" omitted/ omitted/ al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 64/ Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 36, p. 273/ al-Khisal, Vol. 1, p. 257 and Tuhaf al-Uqul, p. 193; "hatta in kanu yuhibbun... ila al-akhir" omitted/ al-Ghaybah by al-Nu'mani, p. 80/ Mustadrak al-Wasa'il, Vol. 17, p. 342/ Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 2, p. 228

entries.arabic: عَنْ جَابِرٍ الْجُعْفِیِّ قَالَ: قَرَأْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِی‌جَعْفَرٍ (علیه السلام) قَوْلَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ لَیْسَ لَکَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَیْءٌ قَالَ بَلَی وَ اللَّهِ إِنَ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَیْئاً وَ شَیْئاً وَ شَیْئاً وَ لَیْسَ حَیْثُ ذَهَبْتَ وَ لَکِنِّی أُخْبِرُکَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی لَمَّا أَمَرَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَنْ یُظْهِرَ وَلَایَهًَْ عَلِیٍّ (علیه السلام) فَکَّرَ فِی عَدَاوَهًْ قَوْمِهِ لَهُ وَ مَعْرِفَتِهِ بِهِمْ وَ ذَلِکَ لِلَّذِی فَضَّلَهُ اللَّهُ بِهِ عَلَیْهِمْ فِی جَمِیعِ خِصَالِهِ کَانَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ آمَنَ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ بِمَنْ أَرْسَلَهُ وَ کَانَ أَنْصَرَ النَّاسِ لِلَّهِ وَ لِرَسُولِهِ وَ أَقْتَلَهُمْ لِعَدُوِّهِمَا وَ أَشَدَّهُمْ بُغْضاً لِمَنْ خَالَفَهُمَا وَ فَضَّلَ عِلْمَهُ الَّذِی لَمْ یُسَاوِهِ أَحَدٌ وَ مَنَاقِبَهُ الَّتِی لَا تُحْصَی شَرَفاً فَلَمَّا فَکَّرَ النَّبِیُّ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِی عَدَاوَهًْ قَوْمِهِ لَهُ فِی هَذِهِ الْخِصَالِ وَ حَسَدِهِمْ لَهُ عَلَیْهَا ضَاقَ عَنْ ذَلِکَ فَأَخْبَرَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَیْسَ لَهُ مِنْ هَذَا الْأَمْرِ شَیْءٌ إِنَّمَا الْأَمْرُ فِیهِ إِلَی اللَّهِ أَنْ یُصَیِّرَ عَلِیّاً (علیه السلام) وَصِیَّهُ وَ وَلِیَّ الْأَمْرِ بَعْدَهُ فَهَذَا عَنَی اللَّهُ وَ کَیْفَ لَا یَکُونُ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَیْءٌ وَ قَدْ فَوَّضَ اللَّهُ إِلَیْهِ أَنْ جَعَلَ مَا أَحَلَّ فَهُوَ حَلَالٌ وَ مَا حَرَّمَ فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ قَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: از جابر جعفی نقل است که گوید: این آی لَیْسَ لَکَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَیْءٌ را خدمت حضرت باقر (علیه السلام) خواندم فرمود: «آری به خدا قسم! برای او از امر مقداری و مقداری و مقداری است آن‌طورکه تو گمان کرده‌ای نیست ولی من برای تو توضیح دهم که خداوند بزرگ وقتی به پیامبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) دستور داد که خلافت و ولایت علی (علیه السلام) را اظهار نماید، پیامبر اکرم (صلی الله علیه و آله) در اندیشه‌ی دشمنی خویشاوندان خود نسبت به علی شد و سابقه‌ای که از آنان داشت و این به آن‌جهت بود که خداوند علی (علیه السلام) را بر تمام آن‌ها در تمام صفات آن برتری بخشیده بود، اوّلین کسی بود که ایمان به پیامبر و رسالت او آورد از تمام مردم بیشتر خدا و پیامبرش را نصرت می‌نمود و از همه بیشتر با دشمنان پیکار داشت و از همه بیشتر کینه با دشمنان خدا و پیامبر داشت و مقام علمی او که هیچ‌کس را یارای هم آوردی با او نبود و سایر مناقبی که از حدّ شمارش بیشتر است. وقتی پیامبر به فکر دشمنی آن‌ها با علی افتاد که بر او رشک می‌برند، دلتنگ شد خداوند به او اطّلاع داد که این امر مربوط به او نیست این بستگی به خدا دارد که علی وصی و ولیّ امر بعد از او باشد منظور از آیه این است چگونه ممکن است او اختیاری نداشته باشد با اینکه به او تفویض نموده که هرچه را حلال کند حلال است و هرچه را حرام کند حرام است به دلیل این آیه ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۰
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۱۱/ العیاشی، ج۱، ص۱۹۷/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۵، ص۳۳۷/ نورالثقلین/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۱۰/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۸۲؛ «بتفاوت»
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Jabir al-Ju'fi reported: I recited the saying of Allah, the Almighty and Majestic, "You have no say in the matter" (3:128) in the presence of Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him). He said: "Yes, by Allah, he indeed has a say in the matter, and a say, and a say. It is not as you have imagined. Rather, I shall inform you that when Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, commanded His Prophet (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) to proclaim the wilayah (guardianship) of Ali (peace be upon him), the Prophet reflected upon the enmity of his people toward him and his knowledge of them. This was because of the excellence Allah had bestowed upon Ali over them in all his qualities: he was the first to believe in the Messenger of Allah (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and in the One who sent him; he was the greatest helper of Allah and His Messenger; he was the most lethal of them to their enemies; he was the most intense in his hatred for those who opposed them; and Allah favored him with knowledge that no one could equal, and virtues that are too numerous to count. When the Prophet (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) reflected upon the enmity of his people toward Ali regarding these qualities and their envy of him for them, he felt distressed. Then Allah informed him that this matter was not for him to decide; rather, the matter regarding it rested with Allah, that He would make Ali (peace be upon him) his successor and the guardian of the affair after him. This is what Allah meant. And how could he not have a say in the matter, when Allah has delegated to him that whatever he declares lawful is lawful, and whatever he declares forbidden is forbidden? He said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you, abstain from it' (59:7)."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 110

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 11; al-Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 197; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 25, p. 337; Nur al-Thaqalayn; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 10; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 382 (with variations).

entries.arabic: عَنْ سَالِمِ‌بْنِ‌عَبْدِ اللَّهِ‌بْنِ‌عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) یَقُولُ أَیُّهَا النَّاسُ عَلِیٌ مِثْلُ حَدِّ السَّیْفِ وَ الصَّابِرُ مَنْ صَبَّرَهُ اللَّهُ یَعْنِی یَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّهًَْ لِمَحَبَّهًْ عَلِیٍّ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ عَلِیَّ‌بْنَ‌أَبِی‌طَالِبٍ (علیه السلام) فِیکُمْ مِثْلُ النَّجْمِ الزَّاهِرِ فِی السَّمَاءِ إِذَا طَلَعَ أَضَاءَ مَا حَوْلَهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ اعْلَمُوا أَنِّی إِنَّمَا قُلْتُ هَذَا لِأَتَقَدَّمَ عَلَیْکُمْ لِیَوْمِ الْوَعِیدِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ {إِنَّهُ} إِذَا کَانَ یَوْمُ الْقِیَامَهًْ حُشِرَ النَّاسُ فِی صَعِیدٍ وَاحِدٍ وَ حُشِرَ عَلِیُّ‌بْنُ‌أَبِی‌طَالِبٍ (علیه السلام) وَسَطَ الْفَوْجِ وَ أَنَا فِی أَوَّلِهِ وَ وُلْدُ عَلِیِّ‌بْنِ‌أَبِی‌طَالِبٍ (علیه السلام) فِی آخِرِ الْفَوْجِ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ فَهَ-لْ رَأَیْتُمْ عَبْداً یَسْبِ-قُ مَوْلَاهُ مَعَاشِرَ النَّ-اسِ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ وَلَایَهًَْ عَلِیٍّ (علیه السلام) فَرْضٌ عَلَیْکُمْ أَحْفَظَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَیْکُمْ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُ جَبْرَئِیلَ (علیه السلام) هَبَطَ بِهِ إِلَیَّ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِینَ مَعَاشِرَ النَّاسِ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّهُ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ {تَعَالَی فِی کِتَابِهِ} ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: سالم‌بن‌عبدالله بن‌عمر از پدرش نقل می‌کند که گفت: از رسول خدا (صلی الله علیه و آله) شنیدم که می‌فرماید: «ای مردم! علی (علیه السلام) مثل لبه‌ی شمشیر است و صبرکننده کسی است که خداوند او را صبور گرداند؛ یعنی به خاطر محبّت علی (علیه السلام) وارد بهشت می‌شود. ای مردم! بدانید که علیّ‌بن‌ابی‌طالب (علیه السلام) در میان شما مانند ستاره‌ی درخشان در آسمان است که وقتی طلوع می‌کند اطرافش را روشن می‌سازد. ای مردم! بدانید که من این مطلب را گفتم تا در روز «وعید» بر شما پیشی‌گیرم. ای مردم! وقتی روز قیامت واقع شود، مردم در یک‌جا جمع شوند و علیّ‌بن‌ابی‌طالب (علیه السلام) در وسط گروه محشور شود و من در اوّل آن هستم و فرزندان علیّ‌بن‌ابی‌طالب در آخر گروه هستند. ای مردم! آیا بنده‌ای را دیده‌اید که بر مولای خود پیشی گی-رد؟ ای مردم ب-دانید که ولای-ت علی (علیه السلام) بر ش-ما واجب است. خداوند آن را بر شما نگه‌داشته است و آن سخن جبرئیل است که آن را از پروردگار جهانیان به‌سوی من فرود آورده است. ای مردم! بدانید که آن سخن خدای تعالی در کتابش است: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۰
entries.references: فرات الکوفی، ص۴۷۵/ بحارالأنوار، ج۳۹، ص۲۳۲؛ «سمعت رسول الله ... لمحبهًْ لی» محذوف
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Salim ibn Abdullah ibn Umar narrates from his father, who said: I heard the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) say: "O people! Ali is like the edge of a sword, and the patient one is he whom God has made patient; meaning, he enters Paradise due to his love for Ali. O people! Know that Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is among you like a brilliant star in the sky; when it rises, it illuminates its surroundings. O people! Know that I have said this so that I may warn you ahead of the Day of Threat. O people! When the Day of Resurrection occurs, people will be gathered on a single plain; Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) will be gathered in the middle of the throng, I will be at its beginning, and the descendants of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) will be at the end of the throng. O people! Have you ever seen a servant who precedes his master? O people! Know that the wilayah (guardianship) of Ali (peace be upon him) is an obligation upon you, which God has preserved for you. It is the word of Gabriel (peace be upon him), who descended with it to me from the Lord of the Worlds. O people! Know that it is the word of God, the Exalted, in His Book: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from.'"

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 110

References: Furat al-Kufi, p. 475; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 39, p. 232; "I heard the Messenger of God... for love of me" [is] omitted.

entries.arabic: عَنْ أَبِی‌جَعْفَرٍ (علیه السلام) قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) بَعْدَ وَفَاهًْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِی الْمَسْجِدِ وَ النَّاسُ مُجْتَمِعُونَ بِصَوْتٍ عَالٍ «الَّذِینَ کَفَرُوا وَ صَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِیلِ اللهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ» فَقَالَ لَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: یَا أَبَا الْحَسَنِ لِمَ قُلْتَ مَا قُلْتَ قَالَ قَرَأْتُ شَیْئاً مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ، قَالَ لَقَدْ قُلْتَهُ لِأَمْرٍ، قَالَ نَعَمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ یَقُولُ فِی کِتَابِهِ «وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا» أَ فَتَشْهَدُ عَلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَنَّهُ اسْتَخْلَفَ فُلَاناً قَالَ: مَا سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَوْصَی إِلَّا إِلَیْکَ، قَالَ: فَهَلَّا بَایَعْتَنِی قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ النَّاسُ عَلَیْهِ فَکُنْتُ مِنْهُمْ، فَقَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) کَمَا اجْتَمَعَ أَهْلُ الْعِجْلِ عَلَی الْعِجْلِ هَاهُنَا فُتِنْتُمْ وَ مَثَلُکُمْ کَمَثَلِ الَّذِی اسْتَوْقَدَ ناراً فَلَمَّا أَضاءَتْ ما حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَ تَرَکَهُمْ فِی ظُلُماتٍ لا یُبْصِرُونَ صُمٌّ بُکْمٌ عُمْیٌ فَهُمْ لا یَرْجِعُونَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۲
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: After the passing of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family), Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him) was in the mosque while the people were gathered, and he said in a loud voice: "Those who disbelieved and hindered [others] from the way of Allah, He has rendered their deeds void" (Muhammad: 1). Ibn Abbas said to him: "O Aba al-Hasan, why did you say what you said?" He replied: "I recited a portion of the Quran." He said: "You surely said it for a reason." He replied: "Yes. Allah says in His Book: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from' (Hashr: 7). Do you bear witness regarding the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) that he appointed so-and-so as his successor?" He [Ibn Abbas] said: "I did not hear the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) make a bequest to anyone except you." He [Ali] said: "Then why did you not pledge allegiance to me?" He said: "The people gathered around him, and I was one of them." Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him) said: "Just as the people of the Calf gathered around the Calf, you have been tested here. Your likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, but when it illuminated what was around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darknesses [so they] could not see. Deaf, dumb, and blind - so they will not return" (Baqarah: 17-18).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 112

References: al-Qummi, Vol. 2, p. 301

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّوَجَلَّ خَلَقَ الْخَلْقَ فَعَلِمَ مَا هُمْ صَائِرُونَ إِلَیْهِ وَ أَمَرَهُمْ وَ نَهَاهُمْ فَمَا أَمَرَهُمْ بِهِ مِنْ شَیْءٍ فَقَدْ جَعَلَ لَهُمُ السَّبِیلَ إِلَی الْأَخْذِ بِهِ وَ مَا نَهَاهُمْ عَنْهُ فَقَدْ جَعَلَ لَهُمُ السَّبِیلَ إِلَی تَرْکِهِ وَ لَا یَکُونُوا آخِذِینَ وَ لَا تَارِکِینَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّه.
entries.persian: خدای عزّوجلّ خلق را آفرید و دانست آنچه را که آن‌ها به‌سوی آن بازمی‌گردند و ایشان را امر فرمود و نهی نمود. پس آنچه را به آن امر فرموده: از هرچه باشد در حقیقت که راه به‌سوی گرفتن و عمل‌کردن به آن را برای ایشان قرار داده و آنچه ایشان را از آن نهی نموده راه به‌سوی ترک آن را برای ایشان قرار داده و عمل‌کنندگان به آن و ترک‌کنندگان نباشند مگر به اذن خدای عزّوجلّ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۲
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah the Almighty and Majestic created the creation and knew what they would return to. He commanded them and forbade them. Whatever He commanded them to do, He has provided them with the means to perform it; and whatever He forbade them from, He has provided them with the means to abandon it. They cannot perform or abandon anything except by the permission of Allah.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 112

References: Al-Tawhid, p. 349 / Nur al-Thaqalayn

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّوَجَلَّ فَوَّضَ إِلَی نَبِیِّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمْرَ خَلْقِهِ لِیَنْظُرَ کَیْفَ طَاعَتُهُمْ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآیَهًَْ- ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.persian: خداوند امور مردم را به پیامبرش واگذاشت تا ببیند چگونه اطاعت می‌کنند بعد همان آیه را قرائت نمود: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۲
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) delegated the affairs of His creation to His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) so that He might observe how they obey him. Then he recited this verse: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 112

References: al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 266

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَأَحْسَنَ أَدَبَهُ فَلَمَّا أَکْمَلَ لَهُ الْأَدَبَ قَالَ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ ثُمَّ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ أَمْرَ الدِّینِ وَ الْأُمَّهًِْ لِیَسُوسَ عِبَادَهُ فَقَالَ عزّوجلّ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) کَانَ مُسَدَّداً مُوَفَّقاً مُؤَیَّداً بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ لَا یَزِلُّ وَ لَا یُخْطِئُ فِی شَیْءٍ مِمَّا یَسُوسُ بِهِ الْخَلْقَ فَتَأَدَّبَ بِآدَابِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ فَرَضَ الصَّلَاهًَْ رَکْعَتَیْنِ رَکْعَتَیْنِ عَشْرَ رَکَعَاتٍ فَأَضَافَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) إِلَی الرَّکْعَتَیْنِ رَکْعَتَیْنِ وَ إِلَی الْمَغْرِبِ رَکْعَهًًْ فَصَارَتْ عَدِیلَ الْفَرِیضَهًِْ لَا یَجُوزُ تَرْکُهُنَّ إِلَّا فِی سَفَرٍ وَ أَفْرَدَ الرَّکْعَهًَْ فِی الْمَغْرِبِ فَتَرَکَهَا قَائِمَهًًْ فِی السَّفَرِ وَ الْحَضَ-رِ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ لَهُ ذَلِکَ کُلَّهُ فَصَارَتِ الْفَرِیضَهًُْ سَبْعَ عَشْرَهًَْ رَکْعَهًًْ ثُمَّ سَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) النَّوَافِلَ أَرْبَعاً وَ ثَلَاثِینَ رَکْعَهًًْ مِثْلَیِ الْفَرِیضَهًِْ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ لَهُ ذَلِکَ وَ الْفَرِیضَهًُْ وَ النَّافِلَهًُْ إِحْدَی وَ خَمْسُونَ رَکْعَهًًْ مِنْهَا رَکْعَتَانِ بَعْدَ الْعَتَمَهًِْ جَالِساً تُعَدُّ بِرَکْعَهًٍْ مَکَانَ الْوَتْرِ وَ فَرَضَ اللَّهُ فِی السَّنَهًِْ صَوْمَ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ وَ سَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) صَوْمَ شَعْبَانَ وَ ثَلَاثَهًَْ أَیَّامٍ فِی کُلِّ شَهْرٍ مِثْلَیِ الْفَرِیضَهًِْ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ لَهُ ذَلِکَ وَ حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ الْخَمْرَ بِعَیْنِهَا وَ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) الْمُسْکِرَ مِنْ کُلِّ شَرَابٍ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ کُلَّهُ وَ عَافَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَشْیَاءَ وَ کَرِهَهَا وَ لَمْ یَنْهَ عَنْهَا نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ إِنَّمَا نَهَی عَنْهَا نَهْیَ إِعَافَهًٍْ وَ کَرَاهَهًٍْ ثُمَّ رَخَّصَ فِیهَا فَصَارَ الْأَخْذُ بِرُخَصِهِ وَاجِباً عَلَی الْعِبَادِ کَوُجُوبِ مَا یَأْخُذُونَ بِنَهْیِهِ وَ عَزَائِمِهِ وَ لَمْ یُرَخِّصْ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِیمَا نَهَاهُمْ عَنْهُ نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ وَ لَا فِیمَا أَمَرَ بِهِ أَمْرَ فَرْضٍ لَازِمٍ فَکَثِیرُ الْمُسْکِرِ مِنَ الْأَشْرِبَهًِْ نَهَاهُمْ عَنْهُ نَهْیَ حَرَامٍ لَمْ یُرَخِّصْ فِیهِ لِأَحَدٍ وَ لَمْ یُرَخِّصْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) لِأَحَدٍ تَقْصِیرَ الرَّکْعَتَیْنِ اللَّتَیْنِ ضَمَّهُمَا إِلَی مَا فَرَضَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ بَلْ أَلْزَمَهُمْ ذَلِکَ إِلْزَاماً وَاجِباً لَمْ یُرَخِّصْ لِأَحَدٍ فِی شَیْءٍ مِنْ ذَلِکَ إِلَّا لِلْمُسَافِرِ وَ لَیْسَ لِأَحَدٍ أَنْ یُرَخِّصَ شَیْئاً مَا لَمْ یُرَخِّصْهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَوَافَقَ أَمْرُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمْرَ اللَّهِ عزّوجلّ وَ نَهْیُهُ نَهْیَ اللَّهِ عزّوجلّ وَ وَجَبَ عَلَی الْعِبَادِ التَّسْلِیمُ لَهُ کَالتَّسْلِیمِ لِلَّهِ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۲
entries.references: الکافی، ج۱، ص۲۶۶/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۴/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) disciplined His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) and perfected his discipline. When He completed his discipline, He said: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character." Then, He delegated to him the affairs of the religion and the community so that he might govern His servants. Thus, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from." And the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) was guided, successful, and supported by the Holy Spirit; he would not slip or err in anything through which he governed the creation, for he was disciplined by the disciplines of Allah.

Then, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) made the prayer obligatory as two rak'ahs by two rak'ahs, totaling ten rak'ahs. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) added two rak'ahs to the [original] two, and one rak'ah to the Maghrib prayer, so they became equivalent to the obligatory [prayers]. It is not permissible to abandon them except while traveling. He kept the single rak'ah in Maghrib, maintaining it both while traveling and while at home. Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) approved all of this for him, and thus the obligatory [prayers] became seventeen rak'ahs.

Then, the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) established the supererogatory prayers (nawafil) as thirty-four rak'ahs, double the amount of the obligatory ones. Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) approved this for him, making the obligatory and the supererogatory prayers fifty-one rak'ahs in total. Among them are two rak'ahs [prayed] sitting after the night prayer (al-'atamah), which are counted as one rak'ah in place of the Witr.

Allah made the fasting of the month of Ramadan obligatory in the year, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) established the fasting of Sha'ban and three days of every month as double the obligatory [fasting]. Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) approved this for him. Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) forbade wine itself, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) forbade every intoxicating drink, and Allah approved all of this for him.

The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) disliked certain things and considered them detestable, but he did not forbid them with the prohibition of the unlawful; rather, he forbade them with the prohibition of dislike and aversion. Then, he granted concessions regarding them, so taking his concessions became obligatory upon the servants, just as it is obligatory to follow his prohibitions and his firm commands. However, the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) did not grant them concessions regarding what he had forbidden them with the prohibition of the unlawful, nor in what he had commanded with the command of a necessary obligation. Thus, he forbade them from the majority of intoxicating drinks with the prohibition of the unlawful, and he did not grant a concession to anyone regarding it. Nor did the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) grant anyone a concession to shorten the two rak'ahs that he added to what Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) had made obligatory; rather, he bound them to that as a necessary obligation. He did not grant anyone a concession in any of that except for the traveler. It is not for anyone to grant a concession in anything that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) has not granted. Thus, the command of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) conforms to the command of Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He), and his prohibition is the prohibition of Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He). It is obligatory upon the servants to submit to him just as they submit to Allah (Blessed and Exalted is He).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 112

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 266; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 4; Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Burhan

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عزّوجلّ أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ عَلَی مَحَبَّتِهِ فَقَالَ وَ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ ثُمَّ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ فَقَالَ عزّوجلّ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ قَالَ عزّوجلّ مَنْ یُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ إِنَّ نَبِیَّ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَوَّضَ إِلَی عَلِیٍّ وَ ائْتَمَنَهُ فَسَلَّمْتُمْ وَ جَحَدَ النَّاسُ فَوَ اللَّهِ لَنُحِبُّکُمْ أَنْ تَقُولُوا إِذَا قُلْنَا وَ أَنْ تَصْمُتُوا إِذَا صَمَتْنَا وَ نَحْنُ فِیمَا بَیْنَکُمْ وَ بَیْنَ اللَّهِ عزّوجلّ مَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِأَحَدٍ خَیْراً فِی خِلَافِ أَمْرِنَا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۴
entries.references: الکافی، ج۱، ص۲۶۵/ المحاسن، ج۱، ص۱۶۲؛ «ما جعل الله ... الی آخر» محذوف/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۳، ص۲۹۵/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۸۴/ فضایل الشیعهًْ، ص۳۴/ الاختصاص، ص۳۳۰/ مستدرک الوسایل، ج۱۷، ص۲۷۲/ البرهان/ نورالثقلین
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) disciplined His Prophet through His love, and thus said: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character" (Qalam/4). Then He delegated [authority] to him, and said (Mighty and Majestic is He): "And whatever the Messenger has given you, take; and what he has forbidden you, refrain from" (Hashr/7). And He said (Mighty and Majestic is He): "He who obeys the Messenger (peace be upon him and his family) has obeyed Allah" (Nisa/80). He then said: The Prophet of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) delegated [authority] to Ali and entrusted him, so you submitted while the people denied. By Allah, we love for you to speak when we speak, and to remain silent when we remain silent. We are the intermediary between you and Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He); Allah has not placed any good for anyone in opposing our command.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 114

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 265; Al-Mahasin, Vol. 1, p. 162; "Ma ja'ala Allah... ila al-akhir" is omitted; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 23, p. 295; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 384; Fada'il al-Shi'ah, p. 34; Al-Ikhtisas, p. 330; Mustadrak al-Wasa'il, Vol. 17, p. 272; Al-Burhan; Nur al-Thaqalayn.

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَی أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَأَحْسَنَ أَدَبَهُ فَقَالَ خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَ أْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَ أَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِینَ فَلَمَّا عَلِمَ أَنَّهُ قَدْ قَبِلَ أَدَبَهُ قَالَ وَ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ فَلَمَّا اسْتَحْکَمَ لَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) مَا أَحَبَّ قَال‌َ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۴
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah the Exalted disciplined His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) and perfected his discipline, saying: "Take to forgiveness, and enjoin kindness, and turn away from the ignorant" (A'raf/199). When He knew that he had accepted His discipline, He said: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character" (Qalam/4). And when that which He loved was firmly established for Him from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family), He said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 114

References: Majmu'at Warram, Vol. 2, p. 27

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَأَحْسَنَ تَأْدِیبَهُ فَقَالَ خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَ أْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَ أَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِینَ فَلَمَّا کَانَ ذَلِکَ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ وَ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ أَمْرَ دِینِهِ فَقَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَحَرَّمَ اللَّهُ الْخَمْرَ بِعَیْنِهَا وَ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) کُلَّ مُسْکِرٍ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ ذَلِکَ وَ کَانَ یَضْمَنُ عَلَی اللَّهِ الْجَنَّهًَْ فَیُجِیزُ اللَّهُ ذَلِکَ لَهُ وَ ذَکَرَ الْفَرَائِضَ فَلَمْ یَذْکُرِ الْجَدَّ فَأَطْعَمَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) سَهْماً فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ ذَلِکَ وَ لَمْ یُفَوِّضْ إِلَی أَحَدٍ مِنَ الْأَنْبِیَاءِ (علیهم السلام) غَیْرِهِ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۶
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۷/ تهذیب الأحکام، ج۹، ص۳۹۷/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۷۸/ وسایل الشیعهًْ، ج۲۶، ص۱۴۲؛ «بتفاوت»/ البرهان/ نورالثقلین
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: The Imams (peace be upon them)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah disciplined His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) and disciplined him well, so He said: "Take to forgiveness, and enjoin kindness, and turn away from the ignorant." When this was fulfilled, Allah revealed: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character." He then delegated to him the affairs of His religion, saying: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from." Thus, Allah forbade wine specifically, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) forbade every intoxicant, and Allah approved of that. He would guarantee Paradise on behalf of Allah, and Allah would approve that for him. He mentioned the obligatory shares of inheritance, but did not mention the grandfather; so the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) granted him a share, and Allah approved of that. He did not delegate this to any of the prophets (peace be upon them) other than him.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 116

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 7; Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Vol. 9, p. 397; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 378; Wasa'il al-Shi'a, Vol. 26, p. 142 (with slight variations); Al-Burhan; Nur al-Thaqalayn

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَدَّبَ نَبِیَّهُ (صلی الله علیه و آله)حَتَّی إِذَا أَقَامَهُ عَلَی مَا أَرَادَ قَالَ لَهُ وَ أْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَ أَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِینَ فَلَمَّا فَعَلَ ذَلِکَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) زَکَّاهُ اللَّهُ فَقَالَ إِنَّکَ لَعَلی خُلُقٍ عَظِیمٍ فَلَمَّا زَکَّاهُ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ دِینَهُ فَقَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَحَرَّمَ اللَّهُ الْخَمْرَ وَ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله)کُلَّ مُسْکِرٍ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ ذَلِکَ کُلَّهُ وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَنْزَلَ الصَّلَاهًَْ وَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَقَّتَ أَوْقَاتِهَا فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۶
entries.references: مستدرک الوسایل، ج۱۷، ص۱۷۳/ بصایرالدرجات، ص۳۸۰/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۰۱، ص۳۴۲/ بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۱۰
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah disciplined His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) until, when he had established him upon what He desired, He said to him: "Enjoin what is right, and turn away from the ignorant" (A'raf/199). When the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) did that, Allah purified him and said: "And indeed, you are of a great moral character" (Qalam/4). When He purified him, He entrusted His religion to him and said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from" (Hashr/7). Thus, Allah forbade wine, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) forbade every intoxicant, and Allah approved all of that. And verily, Allah revealed the prayer, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) set its times, and Allah approved that for him.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 116

References: Mustadrak al-Wasa'il, Vol. 17, p. 173; Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 380; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 101, p. 342; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 10

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی أَدَّبَ مُحَمَّداً (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَلَمَّا تَأَدَّبَ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ فَقَالَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَقَالَ مَنْ یُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ فَکَانَ فِیمَا فَرَضَ فِی الْقُرْآنِ فَرَائِضُ الصُّلْبِ وَ فَرَضَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَرَائِضَ الْجَدِّ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ ذَلِکَ لَهُ وَ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِی الْقُرْآنِ تَحْرِیمَ الْخَمْرِ بِعَیْنِهَا فَحَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) تَحْرِیمَ الْمُسْکِرِ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ فِی أَشْیَاءَ کَثِیرَهًْ فَمَا حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَهُوَ بِمَنْزِلَهًْ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۶
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Verily, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, disciplined Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family), and when he was disciplined, He entrusted [matters] to him. Thus, Blessed and Exalted is He said: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from" (59:7), and He said: "He who obeys the Messenger has obeyed Allah" (4:80). Consequently, among what was made obligatory in the Quran were the obligations regarding the direct descendants (sulb), and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) made obligatory the obligations regarding the grandfather, and Allah authorized that for him. And Allah revealed in the Quran the prohibition of wine itself, then the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) prohibited every intoxicant, and Allah authorized that for him in many things. Therefore, whatever the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) has prohibited is in the same status as what Allah has prohibited.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 116

References: Basa'ir al-Darajat, p. 382

entries.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً (صلی الله علیه و آله) طَاهِراً ثُمَّ أَدَّبَهُ حَتَّی قَوَّمَهُ عَلَی مَا أَرَادَ ثُمَّ فَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِ الْأَمْرَ فَقَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَحَرَّمَ اللَّهُ الْخَمْرَ بِعَیْنِهَا وَ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) الْمُسْکِرَ مِنْ کُلِّ شَرَابٍ وَ فَرَضَ اللَّهُ فَرَائِضَ الصُّلْبِ وَ أَعْطَی رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) الْجَدَّ فَأَجَازَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ وَ أَشْیَاءَ ذَکَرَهَا مِنْ هَذَا الْبَاب.
entries.persian: خداوند، محمّد (صلی الله علیه و آله) را پاک آفرید. سپس به او ادب آموخت تا اینکه او را چنانچه می‌خواست استوار ساخت. سپس امور را به او واگذار کرد و فرمود: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا؛ پس خداوند خود شراب را حرام کرد و رسول خدا (صلی الله علیه و آله) مست‌کننده از هر نوشیدنی را حرام کرد و خداوند واجبات [ارث] فرزندان را واجب نمود و رسول خدا (صلی الله علیه و آله) [از ارث] به جدّ عطا فرمود و خداوند آن را برای او اجازه داد و چیزهایی را از این باب بیان فرمود.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۶
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Verily, Allah created Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) pure, then He disciplined him until He established him upon what He willed. Then He delegated the affair to him, and said: "And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you, abstain [from it]." Thus, Allah forbade wine itself, and the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) forbade the intoxicant from every drink. And Allah ordained the obligatory shares of inheritance for the offspring, and the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) granted [a share] to the grandfather, and Allah permitted that for him, along with other things he mentioned of this nature.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 116

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 11; Nur al-Thaqalayn; al-Burhan

entries.arabic: عَنْ یَاسِرٍ الْخَادِمِ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِلرِّضَا (علیه السلام) مَا تَقُولُ فِی التَّفْوِیضِ فَقَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی فَوَّضَ إِلَی نَبِیِّهِ أَمْرَ دِینِهِ فَقَالَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَأَمَّا الْخَلْقَ وَ الرِّزْقَ فَلا.
entries.persian: یاسر خادم گوید: به امام رضا (علیه السلام) عرض کردم: «درباره‌ی تفویض (اختیار) چه می‌گویی؟ فرمود: خداوند عزّوجلّ اختیار دینش را به پیامبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) عطا کرد و فرمود: ِما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا اما در مورد خلقت و روزی چنین نیست».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۸
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۷، ص۷/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۵، ص۳۲۸/ عیون أخبارالرضا (ج۲، ص۲۰۲/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Yasir the servant said: I said to al-Rida (peace be upon him), "What do you say regarding delegation (tafwid)?" He replied, "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, delegated the affair of His religion to His Prophet, and said: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from.' But as for creation and provision, then no."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 118

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 17, p. 7; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 25, p. 328; Uyun Akhbar al-Rida (Vol. 2, p. 202); Nur al-Thaqalayn; al-Burhan

entries.arabic: عَنْ زُرَارَهًَْ أَنَّهُ قَالَ قُلْتُ لِلصَّادِقِ (علیه السلام) إِنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ وُلْدِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ‌بْنِ‌سَبَإٍ یَقُولُ بِالتَّفْوِیضِ فَقَالَ وَ مَا التَّفْوِیضُ قُلْتُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ عَلِیّاً (علیه السلام) فَفَوَّضَ إِلَیْهِمَا فَخَلَقَا وَ رَزَقَا وَ أَمَاتَا وَ أَحْیَیَا فَقَالَ (علیه السلام) کَذَبَ عَدُوُّ اللَّهِ إِذَا انْصَرَفْتَ إِلَیْهِ فَاتْلُ عَلَیْهِ هَذِهِ الْآیَهًَْ الَّتِی فِی سُورَهًِْ الرَّعْدِ أَمْ جَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَکاءَ خَلَقُوا کَخَلْقِهِ فَتَشابَهَ الْخَلْقُ عَلَیْهِمْ قُلِ اللَّهُ خالِقُ کُلِّ شَیْءٍ وَ هُوَ الْواحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ فَانْصَرَفْتُ إِلَی الرَّجُلِ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ فَکَأَنِّی أَلْقَمْتُهُ حَجَراً أَوْ قَالَ فَکَأَنَّمَا خَرِسَ وَ قَدْ فَوَّضَ اللَّهُ عزّوجلّ إِلَی نَبِیِّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمْرَ دِینِهِ فَقَالَ عزّوجلّ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ قَدْ فَوَّضَ ذَلِکَ إِلَی الْأَئِمَّهًِْ (علیهم السلام).
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: Zurarah said: I said to Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), "A man from the descendants of Abdullah ibn Saba' believes in 'tafwid' (delegation)." He asked, "And what is tafwid?" I said, "That Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, created Muhammad (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and Ali (peace be upon him), and then delegated to them, so they created, provided sustenance, caused death, and gave life." He (peace be upon him) said, "The enemy of Allah lies! When you return to him, recite to him this verse which is in Surah al-Ra'd: 'Or have they attributed to Allah partners who created like His creation so that the creation seemed similar to them? Say, "Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the One, the Prevailing."' (13:16). I returned to the man and informed him, and it was as if I had stuffed a stone into his mouth—or he said, 'It was as if he had been struck dumb.' Indeed, Allah (Mighty and Majestic is He) has delegated the matter of His religion to His Prophet (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family), for He (Mighty and Majestic is He) said: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you—take; and what he has forbidden you—refrain from.' And He has delegated that to the Imams (peace be upon them)."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 118

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 25, p. 343

entries.arabic: یَا ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ وَ کَیْفَ تَنَامُ عَیْنَا قَلْبٍ مَشْغُولٍ یَا ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ مَلِکُ جَوَارِحِکَ قَلْبُکَ فَإِذَا أَدْهَاهُ أَمْرٌ طَارَ النَّوْمُ عَنْهُ هَا أَنَا ذَا کَمَا تَرَی مِنْ أَوَّلِ اللَّیْلِ اعْتَرَانِی الْفِکْرُ وَ السَّهَرُ لِمَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ نَقْضِ عَهْدِ أَوَّلِ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّهًْ الْمُقَدَّرِ عَلَیْهَا نَقْضُ عَهْدِهَا إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمَرَ مَنْ أَمَرَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ بِالسَّلَامِ عَلَیَّ فِی حَیَاتِهِ بِإِمْرَهًْ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ فَکُنْتُ أُؤَکِّدُ أَنْ أَکُونَ کَذَلِکَ بَعْدَ وَفَاتِهِ یَا ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَا أَوْلَی النَّاسِ بِالنَّاسِ بَعْدَهُ وَ لَکِنْ أُمُورٌ اجْتَمَعَتْ عَلَی رَغْبَهًْ النَّاسِ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ أَمْرِهَا وَ نَهْیِهَا وَ صَرْفِ قُلُوبِ أَهْلِهَا عَنِّی وَ أَصْلُ ذَلِکَ مَا قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّوَجَلَّ فِی کِتَابِهِ أَمْ یَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلی ما آتاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَقَدْ آتَیْنا آلَ إِبْراهِیمَ الْکِتابَ وَ الْحِکْمَةَ وَ آتَیْناهُمْ مُلْکاً عَظِیماً فَلَوْ لَمْ یَکُنْ ثَوَابٌ وَ لَا عِقَابٌ لَکَانَ بِتَبْلِیغِ الرَّسُولِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَرْضٌ عَلَی النَّاسِ اتِّبَاعُهُ وَ اللَّهُ عَزَّوَجَلَّ یَقُولُ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۸
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: O Ibn Abbas, how can the eyes of a heart that is preoccupied sleep? O Ibn Abbas, the ruler of your limbs is your heart; when a grave matter occupies it, sleep flees from it. I am as you see me: from the beginning of the night, I have been overcome by thought and wakefulness due to the breach of covenant by the first of this Ummah, whose breach of covenant was destined for them. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) commanded those of his companions whom he commanded during his lifetime to greet me as the Commander of the Faithful, and I insisted on remaining as such after his passing. O Ibn Abbas, I am the most entitled of people to the people after him, but matters converged upon the people's desire for the world, its commands and prohibitions, and the turning of the hearts of its people away from me. The root of this is what Allah the Almighty said in His Book: "Or do they envy the people for what Allah has given them of His bounty? For We had already given the family of Abraham the Scripture and wisdom and conferred upon them a great kingdom." Even if there were no reward or punishment, it would have been an obligation upon the people to follow the Messenger (peace be upon him and his family) due to his proclamation. Allah the Almighty says: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 118

References: al-Yaqin, p. 321; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 29, p. 549

entries.arabic: عَرَفْتُمْ فِی مُنْکِرِینَ کَثِیرٍ وَ أَحْبَبْتُمْ فِی مُبْغِضِینَ کَثِیرٍ وَقَدْ یَکُونُ حُبّاً لِلَّهِ فِی اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) وَ حُبّاً فِی الدُّنْیَا فَمَا کَانَ فِی اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَثَوَابُهُ عَلَی اللَّهِ وَ مَا کَانَ لِلدُّنْیَا فَلَیْسَ شَیْءٌ ثُمَّ نَفَضَ یَدَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ هَذِهِ الْمُرْجِئَهًَْ وَ هَذِهِ الْقَدَرِیَّهًَْ وَ هَذِهِ الْخَوَارِجَ لَیْسَ مِنْهُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا یَرَی أَنَّهُ عَلَی الْحَقِّ وَ إِنَّکُمْ إِنَّمَا أَجَبْتُمُونَا فِی اللَّهِ ثُمَّ تَلَا أَطِیعُوا اللهَ وَ أَطِیعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَ أُولِی الْأَمْرِ مِنْکُمْ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ مَنْ یُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ إِنْ کُنْتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللهَ فَاتَّبِعُونِی یُحْبِبْکُمُ اللهُ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۱۸
entries.references: مستدرک الوسایل، ج۱۲، ص۲۱۸/ العیاشی، ج۱، ص۱۶۷؛ «و من یطع الرسول ... الی آخر» محذوف/ بحارالأنوار، ج۲۷، ص۹۴/ بحارالأنوار، ج۶۵، ص۹۰ و المحاسن، ج۱، ص۱۵۶؛ «و من یطع الرسول ... الی آخر» محذوف و «عرفتم فی المنکرین کثیر ... نفض یده» محذوف
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: You have recognized [the truth] while many have denied it, and you have loved [us] while many have hated it. Sometimes love is for the sake of Allah, in Allah, and for His Messenger (peace be upon him and his family), and sometimes it is for the sake of the world. Whatever is for the sake of Allah and His Messenger (peace be upon him and his family), its reward is with Allah; and whatever is for the sake of the world, it is nothing. He then shook his hand and said: There is not one among these Murji'ah, these Qadariyyah, and these Khawarij who does not believe that they are upon the truth. But you have responded to us for the sake of Allah. He then recited: "Obey Allah and obey the Messenger and those in authority among you. And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from. And whoever obeys the Messenger has obeyed Allah" (Nisa/59, 80); "If you should love Allah, then follow me, [so] Allah will love you" (Al-Imran/31).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 118

References: Mustadrak al-Wasa'il, Vol. 12, p. 218; al-Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 167 (with the portion "And whoever obeys the Messenger..." omitted); Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 27, p. 94; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 65, p. 90; al-Mahasin, Vol. 1, p. 156 (with the portion "And whoever obeys the Messenger..." and "You have recognized... shook his hand" omitted).

entries.arabic: سَارِعُوا فِی طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ فَوَ الَّذِی نَفْسِی بِیَدِهِ لَحَدِیثٌ وَاحِدٌ فِی حَلَالٍ وَ حَرَامٍ تَأْخُذُهُ عَنْ صَادِقٍ خَیْرٌ مِنَ الدُّنْیَا وَ مَا حَمَلَتْ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ وَ فِضَّهًٍْ وَ ذَلِکَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ یَقُولُ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَ إِنْ کَانَ عَلِیٌّ لَیَأْمُرُ بِقِرَاءَهًِْ الْمُصْحَفِ.
entries.persian: در کسب دانش بشتابید! سوگند به آنکه جانم به دست اوست هر آینه یک حدیث در مورد حلال و حرام که از شخص امین و راستگویی بگیری از دنیا و آنچه طلا و نقره در آن است بهتر است، و اینجاست که خدا می‌فرماید: ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا، و علی (علیه السلام) پیوسته به قرائت قرآن فرمان می‌داد.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۲۰
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۲، ص۱۴۶/ المحاسن، ج۱، ص۲۲۷/ نورالثقلین/ مستطرفات السرایر، ص۶۴۵؛ «تنازعوا» بدل «سارعوا»
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Hasten to seek knowledge, for by Him in whose hand is my soul, a single hadith regarding the lawful and the unlawful that you take from a truthful person is better than the world and all the gold and silver it contains. This is because God says: "And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from." And Ali (peace be upon him) used to command the recitation of the Mushaf (Quran).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 120

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 2, p. 146 / Al-Mahasin, Vol. 1, p. 227 / Nur al-Thaqalayn / Mustatrafat al-Sara'ir, p. 645; "Tanaza'u" (strive/compete) instead of "Sari'u" (hasten).

entries.arabic: قَالَ أَمِیرُالْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) بَعْدَ وَفَاهًِْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فِی الْمَسْجِدِ وَ النَّاسُ مُجْتَمِعُونَ بِصَوْتٍ عَالٍ الَّذِینَ کَفَرُوا وَ صَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِیلِ اللهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ فَقَالَ ابْنُ‌عَبَّاسٍ (رحمة الله علیه) یَا أَبَا الْحَسَنِ (علیه السلام) لِمَ قُلْتَ مَا قُلْتَ قَالَ قَرَأْتُ شَیْئاً مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ قَالَ لَقَدْ قُلْتَهُ لِأَمْرٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ، إِنَّ اللَّهَ یَقُولُ فِی کِتَابِهِ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا، فَتَشْهَدُ عَلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَنَّهُ اسْتَخْلَفَ أَبَا بَکْرٍ قَالَ مَا سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَوْصَی إِلَّا إِلَیْکَ. قَالَ فَهَلَّا بَایَعْتَنِی قَالَ اجْتَمَعَ النَّاسُ عَلَی أَبِی‌بَکْرٍ فَکُنْتُ مِنْهُمْ. فَقَالَ أَمِیرُالْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام)کَمَا اجْتَمَعَ أَهْلُ الْعِجْلِ عَلَی الْعِجْلِ، هَاهُنَا فُتِنْتُمْ، وَ مَثَلُکُمْ کَمَثَلِ الَّذِی اسْتَوْقَدَ ناراً فَلَمَّا أَضاءَتْ ما حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَ تَرَکَهُمْ فِی ظُلُماتٍ لا یُبْصِرُونَ صُمٌّ بُکْمٌ عُمْیٌ فَهُمْ لا یَرْجِعُونَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۲۰
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۲۹، ص۱۹/ بحارالأنوار، ج۶۷، ص۳۲۱؛ «بتفاوت»
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/7)

English translation: After the passing of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family), the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) stood in the mosque while the people were gathered and said in a loud voice: "Those who disbelieved and hindered [others] from the way of Allah, He has rendered their deeds void." (Muhammad: 1). Ibn Abbas (may Allah have mercy on him) said to him, "O Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him), why did you say what you said?" He replied, "I recited a portion of the Quran." He asked, "Did you say it for a specific reason?" He replied, "Yes. Allah says in His Book: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you, take; and what he has forbidden you, refrain from.' (Hashr: 7). Do you bear witness that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) appointed Abu Bakr as his successor?" He replied, "I have not heard the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) make a bequest to anyone except you." He asked, "Then why did you not pledge allegiance to me?" He replied, "The people gathered around Abu Bakr, and I was one of them." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said, "Just as the people of the Calf gathered around the Calf, you have been tested here. Your likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, but when it illuminated what was around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darknesses [so they] could not see. Deaf, dumb, and blind—so they will not return." (Baqarah: 17-18).

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 120

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 29, p. 19; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 67, p. 321 (with variations).

entries.arabic: عَنْ مُوسَی‌بْنِ‌أَشْیَمَ قَالَ: کُنْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِی‌عَبْدِ‌اللَّهِ (علیه السلام) إِذْ أَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ فَسَأَلَهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثاً فِی مَقْعَدٍ فَقَالَ أَبُوعَبْدِ‌اللَّهِ (علیه السلام) قَدْ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ بِثَلَاثٍ ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ آخَرُ فَسَأَلَهُ عَنْ تِلْکَ الْمَسْأَلَهًِْ بِعَیْنِهَا فَقَالَ لَیْسَ بِطَلَاقٍ فَأَظْلَمَ عَلَیَّ الْبَیْتُ لِمَا رَأَیْتُ مِنْهُ فَالْتَفَتَ إِلَیَّ فَقَالَ یَا ابْنَ أَشْیَمَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَوَّضَ الْمُلْکَ إِلَی سُلَیْمَانَ فَقَالَ هذا عَطاؤُنا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِکْ بِغَیْرِ حِسابٍ وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَوَّضَ إِلَی مُحَمَّدٍ (صلی الله علیه و آله) أَمْرَ دِینِهِ فَقَالَ وَ ما آتاکُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاکُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا فَمَا کَانَ مُفَوَّضاً إِلَی مُحَمَّدٍ (صلی الله علیه و آله) فَقَدْ فُوِّضَ إِلَیْنَا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۱۶، ص۱۲۰
entries.references: وسایل الشیعهًْ، ج۲۲، ص۷۰/ الکافی، ج۱، ص۲۶۵ و الاختصاص، ص۳۳۰ و نورالثقلین؛ «علی بن ابراهیم ... تقیه قال» بدل «کنت عند ابی عبدالله ... رأیت منه»/ البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Hashr/ 7)

English translation: Musa ibn Ashyam said: I was with Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him) when a man came to him and asked him about a man who had divorced his wife three times in one sitting. Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him) said, "She has become irrevocably separated from him by the three." Then another man came to him and asked him about that very same issue, and he said, "It is not a divorce." The room became dark to me because of what I had seen from him. He turned to me and said, "O son of Ashyam, indeed Allah delegated authority to Solomon and said: 'This is Our gift, so give or withhold without account' (Sad: 39). And indeed, Allah delegated the affair of His religion to Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) and said: 'And whatever the Messenger has given you, take; and whatever he has forbidden you, refrain from it' (Hashr: 7). So, whatever was delegated to Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) has been delegated to us."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 16, p. 120

References: Wasa'il al-Shi'ah, Vol. 22, p. 70; al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 265; al-Ikhtisas, p. 330; Nur al-Thaqalayn; "Ali ibn Ibrahim ... taqiyyah qala" instead of "kuntu 'inda Abi Abd Allah ... ra'aytu minhu"; al-Burhan
